description: Using Tammy's Head fires 10 Brimstone lasers in all directions simultaneously. Each laser deals your full Brimstone damage. One of the most powerful combos in the game, capable of clearing entire rooms and melting bosses instantly.

The Technology laser coils around the Brimstone beam, granting a 1.5x damage multiplier. The Tech laser also fires continuously while Brimstone charges, giving you damage output during the charge phase.
Fires a large Brimstone ring instead of the normal Tech X laser ring. The ring has a wider hitbox and deals Brimstone's full damage. One of the highest DPS combinations in the game.
The knife gains a Brimstone charge. Releasing fires the main knife plus a barrage of smaller knives that cross the entire screen, each dealing 2x Isaac's damage. Devastating at any range.

The Brimstone beam poisons enemies and creates explosions on contact. The +40 damage from Ipecac applies to each tick of the beam, massively increasing total output. Self-damage from explosions is possible.
Each Epic Fetus missile explosion shoots 10 Brimstone lasers in all directions. Combines the missile's massive base damage with full Brimstone beams. Absolutely devastating room clear.
Every bomb explosion fires 10 Brimstone lasers in all directions. Turns every bomb into a room-clearing event. Works with any source of bombs.
The Brimstone laser gains homing, curving toward enemies. Massively improves consistency in rooms with mobile enemies or awkward layouts where a straight beam would miss.
Fires 2 Brimstone beams in a slight spread. 20/20 doesn't apply a fire rate penalty, so you get double beams at normal charge speed. Clean double damage.
Fires 3 Brimstone beams in a spread. The fire rate penalty from Inner Eye increases charge time, but triple beams still massively increase DPS.
Fires 4 Brimstone beams in a wide spread. Huge area coverage and damage output, though the fire rate penalty makes the charge noticeably slower.
Brimstone becomes variable-charge. You can release early for a thin weak beam or fully charge for a massive beam at 250% damage and 160% width. Adds tactical flexibility.
Brimstone takes priority but fires 3-5 additional beams in random directions alongside the main beam. Huge room coverage without Lung's fire rate penalty.
The beam gradually increases in size and damage the farther it travels, gaining +20% damage per tile of distance. Makes long-range beams deal significantly more than close-range ones.
Beams bounce off walls once, creating a second beam at full damage. Effectively doubles your coverage in enclosed rooms. The bounced beam can hit enemies the original missed.
The Brimstone laser travels through walls and reappears from the opposite side. Lets you hit enemies through obstacles and in rooms with complex layouts.
Enemies are pulled toward the beam while it fires. Keeps enemies in the beam's path for more damage ticks and prevents them from dodging. Also moves pickups.
Spawns split tears when the beam hits the first enemy or obstacle. Adds splash damage around the beam's impact point.
Spawns two perpendicular beams at the first point where Brimstone contacts an enemy or wall. Creates a T-shaped attack pattern for better coverage.
The beam destroys enemy projectiles during each damage tick. Provides a defensive benefit while attacking, especially useful against bullet-hell bosses.
Brimstone fires automatically at full charge and continues indefinitely while holding fire. You can steer the beam direction with the joystick. Tears stat increases beam damage and width.
Similar to Soy Milk Ã¢â‚¬â€ fires automatically at full charge with indefinite beam duration. Gains random worm trinket visual effects. Tears stat boosts damage and width.
Creates a controllable crosshair. Brimstone fires automatically at full charge and curves toward the crosshair, letting you steer the beam after firing.
Brimstone fires automatically toward the crosshair at full charge. Frees up your movement to dodge while the beam fires directionally.
Tech.5 fires its random laser bursts while you charge Brimstone. Gives you constant chip damage during the charge phase so you're never doing zero DPS.
The Technology 2 continuous laser fires while you charge Brimstone. Similar to Tech.5 but constant instead of random. Steady damage during charge.
The beam wraps around Isaac in a circle once, then shoots straight. Creates an orbital attack zone before the beam extends. Unique but situational.
Upgrades Azazel's short-range Mini Brimstone into the full-length Brimstone laser. Removes Azazel's biggest weakness (short range) while keeping flight and black hearts.
The knife deals Polyphemus-boosted damage (damage x2 + 4). Since the knife already has high base damage and hits multiple times per throw, this creates absurd single-target DPS.
Each missile explosion shoots 10 knives in a circle from the impact point. Combines massive missile damage with a ring of full-damage knives.
At full charge, fires 3-5 additional knives in random directions alongside the main knife. Massive burst of knives fills the room.
Isaac holds one knife but shoots multiple at their typical spread. Double knives without a fire rate penalty. Clean DPS increase.
Throws 3 knives in a spread. The fire rate penalty increases charge time but triple knives devastate rooms.
The knife deals triple damage regardless of range. Proptosis's distance penalty doesn't apply to the knife, making this a pure 3x damage multiplier.
Dead Eye's damage multiplier increases with consecutive hits. Since the knife almost never misses (it returns through enemies), you maintain max stacks permanently for a consistent 2x multiplier.
Isaac controls a floating knife that deals 2x damage per tick. No charge needed Ã¢â‚¬â€ just steer it into enemies for continuous high damage.
Enemies are pulled toward the knife, both while held and while thrown. Keeps targets in the knife's path for maximum hit count.
The knife blocks enemy projectiles even while held in front of Isaac. Acts as a permanent shield against bullets while you charge.
The knife creates bone shards while in contact with an enemy, adding splash damage around each hit.
Both fire normally Ã¢â‚¬â€ the knife throws alongside a laser beam connected to it. Double attack methods with full damage on each.
The knife fires with a Tech X ring around it. Both the knife and ring deal damage independently, combining melee and ring damage.
Missiles shoot 10 Technology laser beams in all directions on explosion, with a +25 damage bonus. Turns each missile into a laser nova.
Fires 2 Tech X rings instead of one. Slight damage reduction per ring but nearly doubles total output.
Tech X rings shoot out smaller split tears on hit. Adds splash damage around each ring impact.
Tech X rings block enemy projectiles as they travel across the room. Provides offensive and defensive capability simultaneously.
The rings bounce off walls once, extending their travel distance and allowing them to hit enemies behind obstacles.
Rings bend inward toward enemies, substantially increasing the damage area as the ring curves to hit targets.
Fires 3-5 extra rings in a 30-degree spread alongside the main ring. Massive area coverage though each ring has reduced damage.
Dr. Fetus bombs explode on contact, become poison clouds, and deal massive combined damage. Isaac gains immunity to his own bomb shots. One of the safest Ipecac combos.
Pyromaniac heals Isaac from Ipecac's self-damage explosions. Turns Ipecac's main drawback into self-healing, making you nearly unkillable while dealing +40 damage per shot.
Host Hat grants immunity to Ipecac's self-damage explosions. Removes Ipecac's main risk without the healing benefit of Pyromaniac.
Missiles become green and poisonous with drastically increased damage and larger blast radius. Poison gas clouds linger on impact. Devastating area denial.
Fires a barrage of Ipecac shots in a spread. Each deals +40 damage and poisons. Overlapping explosions devastate everything nearby. Extreme self-damage risk Ã¢â‚¬â€ stay far from enemies.
The main Ipecac tear splits into 4 explosive splash tears on hit. Each split tear deals full explosive damage, multiplying the total output.
Ipecac tear spawns 2 smaller explosive tears on collision with walls or enemies. The split tears chain-explode for massive area damage.
The controllable tear gains Ipecac's +40 damage, explodes when killing an enemy, and returns to Isaac after each kill. High damage with good control.
Creates a poisonous laser that explodes on enemy or obstacle contact. High damage but explosions can hurt Isaac if enemies are too close.
WARNING: Ipecac shots boomerang back toward Isaac and explode on contact. Extremely likely to self-damage. One of the most dangerous combinations in the game.
Very fast fire rate fills the screen with low-damage Ipecac explosions. Damage per shot is heavily reduced but the sheer volume compensates. Self-damage risk is extreme.

Libra rebalances stats after Soy Milk's tears up / damage down. The massive fire rate gets redistributed across all stats, resulting in high damage, speed, and range while keeping very high fire rate.
Fires tear barrages continuously without needing to charge. The rapid auto-fire combined with Lung's spread creates a constant stream of projectile bursts.
The continuous Technology 2 laser ticks much faster due to Soy Milk's tears up, dramatically increasing laser DPS while also firing rapid soy shots.
The rapid stream of tears blocks nearly all incoming enemy projectiles. Creates an almost impenetrable defensive wall in front of Isaac.
Enemies get constantly pulled toward the stream of rapid-fire tears, improving accuracy and preventing them from dodging.
Each tiny tear gains significant damage at range from Lump of Coal. Since Soy Milk fires so many tears, the long-range damage bonus adds up quickly.
The extra tear cluster from Lead Pencil fires very frequently due to the high fire rate, adding bursts of full-damage tears between the rapid soy shots.
Rock Bottom locks in the high fire rate before Soy Milk's damage penalty applies. Results in massive fire rate with no damage loss. Game-breaking if found before Soy Milk.
Each missile triggers Sad Bombs on explosion, releasing a burst of tears in all directions. The tears inherit your damage and tear effects, adding massive extra damage per strike.
Isaac becomes immune to his own airstrikes and heals from them instead. Removes all self-damage risk and turns missed shots into healing opportunities.
Grants immunity to your own missile explosions. Removes the self-damage risk from Epic Fetus entirely.
Increased tear delay but massively increases missile blast radius and damage. Each missile becomes a devastating area bomb.
Missiles explode in a large cross-shaped blast pattern, each explosion dealing 0.75x damage. Dramatically increases the area of effect.
2-4 additional crosshairs orbit the main crosshair. Missiles fire onto each crosshair simultaneously, creating a carpet-bombing effect.
Both fire normally Ã¢â‚¬â€ you get missiles AND a Technology laser. Explosions also shoot 10 Technology beams in all directions.
Fires two tears with no fire rate penalty, but the damage bonus becomes a flat +5 instead of multiplicative. Still a net positive for double shots.
If Rock Bottom is obtained first, Isaac keeps his original fire rate while receiving Polyphemus's massive damage boost. Eliminates the main downside entirely.
Bombs grow in size and damage. After the first bomb explodes, a second bomb appears at the explosion point dealing half damage. Double the explosions.
Tears gain both Sacred Heart's homing and Godhead's damaging aura. Homing ensures tears track enemies while the aura deals continuous damage. Both damage multipliers stack multiplicatively.
Both grant homing Ã¢â‚¬â€ Sacred Heart's homing stacks with Spoon Bender for even more aggressive tracking. The 2.3x damage multiplier from Sacred Heart makes every homing tear devastating.
The controllable tear increases in size and gains Godhead's damaging aura. Park it on enemies for continuous aura + contact damage. Also gains homing in Repentance.
The damaging aura increases in size and damage the longer the tear is airborne. Long-range tears gain massive auras that shred anything nearby.
Soy Milk's damage penalty doesn't affect Godhead's aura damage. The rapid stream of homing tears each carry damaging auras, filling the room with overlapping damage zones.
Reduces tear delay by 20% and each tear in the barrage becomes a charged shot. Greatly increased fire rate with higher damage per tear. Very strong synergy.
Fires 12 Technology lasers in a 30-degree spread that arc across the room. No tear delay penalty applied. Room-clearing laser fan.
Fires 6 bombs simultaneously that detonate together. Multi-shot items add extra bombs. Carpet-bombs the room.
Your own bomb shots heal you instead of dealing self-damage. Turns Dr. Fetus into a safe healing weapon. Fire freely at close range.
Bomb projectiles home toward enemies. Removes the need to aim precisely, making Dr. Fetus much more consistent.
Bombs explode in a large cross-shaped pattern. Dramatically increases the area of effect of each shot.
Each bomb shot releases tears in all directions when it explodes. Adds significant splash damage to every shot.
Bombs split into 4 tiny bombs on explosion that detonate almost immediately. Quadruples the explosive coverage.
Bombs split into 2 additional bombs on explosion that detonate almost instantly. Creates chain-explosion effects.
Kidney Stone periodically fires a high-damage stone with a burst of tears. With Chocolate Milk, the stone shot gains the charge damage bonus for even more damage.
Fully charged shots deal massive Polyphemus-boosted damage. You can also tap-fire for rapid weak shots. Enormous damage ceiling with a flexible floor.
Charging increases the Godhead aura size and damage. Fully charged shots carry huge damaging auras that melt enemies on contact.
The controllable tear pulls all enemies toward it magnetically. Drag enemies into hazards or hold them in place for continuous contact damage.
Forms a large controllable Brimstone ring. No charge needed. Deals 15 damage ticks per second. Enemies inside the ring without touching it aren't hurt. Steer it onto enemies for massive DPS.
Kamikaze provides unlimited explosions. With Pyromaniac, each explosion heals Isaac instead of dealing damage. Infinite healing on demand.
When Isaac takes damage, troll bombs spawn. With Pyromaniac, those bombs heal Isaac, negating the initial damage and providing extra healing. Self-sustaining defense.
Blood Rights costs half a red heart per use, but Isaac's Heart makes you invulnerable to self-damage. Free full-room damage dealer with zero cost every use.
Stand still to become invulnerable via Gnawed Leaf. Daddy Longlegs stomps enemies automatically. Slow but completely safe Ã¢â‚¬â€ you can AFK every room and boss.
Become invulnerable while stationary. Cube of Meat orbits and deals contact damage to enemies that approach. Slow but completely safe room clears.
Become invulnerable while stationary. Ball of Bandages orbits and deals contact damage. Same principle as Cube of Meat Ã¢â‚¬â€ safe but slow.
Incubus fires tears independently of Isaac. While in Gnawed Leaf statue form, Incubus still shoots, providing offense while you're invulnerable.
Tears split on hit AND on collision with obstacles. Each split tear then splits again, creating a cascade of projectiles that fills the room. Combined with any tears up, this becomes screen-clearing.
Both items create split tears on impact. The splits stack, creating even more projectile spread. Excellent crowd control in dense rooms.
The controllable Ludovico tear counts as consecutive hits while touching enemies. Quickly builds Dead Eye to max stacks for doubled damage.
Crown of Light's 2x damage multiplier applies to Brimstone's already high damage. As long as you haven't taken damage this room, the beam deals double damage.
The Lost has no red hearts, so Crown of Light's 2x damage multiplier is always active as long as Holy Mantle hasn't broken this room. Permanent double damage in most rooms.
Blank Card activates your held card/rune twice instead of once. Doubles the effect of powerful cards like Jera (infinite pickups), Perthro (double rerolls), or Algiz (extended invulnerability).
Placebo activates your held pill twice. Doubles the effect of stat-up pills like Tears Up, Damage Up, etc. Strong for snowballing stats.
Fires 6 tears at once (2x3). While the tears down from Inner Eye applies, the sheer number of projectiles makes this very strong, especially with piercing or spectral tears.
Fires 8 tears at once (2x4). Massive projectile count covers a huge area. Fire rate penalty from Mutant Spider is noticeable but compensated by volume.
Lead Pencil triggers approximately every 2 seconds while firing the Brimstone beam. Adds bursts of full-damage tears alongside the continuous laser.
Lead Pencil fires tear bursts periodically while the Ludovico tear is out. Gives you a secondary attack alongside the controllable tear.
Eve's Mascara doubles damage and halves fire rate. 20/20 doubles shots without a fire rate penalty. Net result: double damage with double shots at half speed Ã¢â‚¬â€ 2x total DPS increase.
The Brimstone beam starts at 3x damage up close but decreases with distance. In Repentance, the penalty is capped at a minimum. Still very strong for close-range fights.
Distance penalty is removed for Dr. Fetus bombs. All bombs get the damage multiplier regardless of range. Pure damage increase.
Spirit Sword overrides Brimstone. Brimstone's stats apply but you swing the sword instead of firing a laser. Generally considered a downgrade.
Revelation's holy laser gains poison damage and explodes multiple times along its path. Adds significant extra damage to an already powerful attack.
Four fetus tears fire alongside the Brimstone beam. With Soy Milk or Almond Milk, the fetuses fire continuously. Adds extra damage sources to Brimstone.
Fetus tears carry small spinning knives that deal extra contact damage on enemy hits. Adds knife damage to each fetus projectile.
Fetus tears explode on contact, poisoning enemies and dealing Ipecac's bonus damage. Each fetus becomes a homing explosive.
All new item pedestals cycle between two options. Massively increases your chances of finding strong items by giving you a choice on every pedestal.
Prevents unhelpful items (those with the nolostbr tag) from appearing and automatically re-rolls them. Ensures every item you see is useful for The Lost's build.
Book of Belial becomes a passive item that sits beneath your active item. Damage bonus scales with active item charges. Permanent damage up that frees the active slot.
Whore of Babylon activates regardless of health. Dead Bird activates without taking damage. Both effects become permanent, giving Eve constant damage bonuses and a free familiar.
Brimstone beam becomes much wider with 2x larger collision area. Makes Azazel's already strong short-range beam cover a huge area.
Grants one red Heart Container and increases maximum heart limit to 18. Allows Magdalene to stack even more health for her tank playstyle.
Whenever a red Heart Container would have been gained, grants 2 Soul Hearts instead of 1. Doubles the value of every heart container pickup for ???.
Bloody Lust can grant 4 more damage boosts at +0.2 intervals, raising the maximum total to +14.0 damage. Makes Samson's rage mechanic scale even higher.
On death as Lazarus, Lazarus Risen gains +7.2 damage that fades at 0.12 per second. Killing enemies restores 0.07 damage. Rewards aggressive play after resurrection.
Creates 3 random items from random item pools Ã¢â‚¬â€ you can only take one. A free high-quality item choice that can define or save a run.
Grants +1 Luck and nearly every floor spawns an upgraded Arcade with more machines, even without 5 coins. Great for resource generation.
Adds one Coin Heart and raises maximum Coin Heart limit to 4. More health capacity for a character who desperately needs it.
Any 3 Guppy items trigger the Guppy transformation: grants flight and spawns Blue Flies on every tear hit. With high fire rate, you generate a massive army of flies that deal your damage.
Any 3 Guppy items trigger the transformation. Dead Cat + Guppy's Collar + Guppy's Head is a common path. The fly generation from Guppy far outweighs the health loss from Dead Cat.
Guppy's Paw converts red heart containers to Soul Hearts. Blue Baby has no red hearts, so the paw has no effect. Useless pickup for this character.
Dead Cat gives 9 extra lives but sets health to 1. The Lost already has 0 health, so he just gets 9 extra lives with no downside. Also counts toward Guppy.
Dead Cat sets health to 1 heart container. For Keeper, this means 1 Coin Heart Ã¢â‚¬â€ a significant downgrade from his normal 2-3. The extra lives partially compensate but it's risky.
Brimstone's charge-up leaves The Lost vulnerable, but the massive damage per shot means fewer total shots needed per room. With Holy Mantle, the charge time is manageable.
Mom's Knife's high damage-per-hit is ideal for The Lost's glass-cannon playstyle. Kill enemies before they can threaten you. The knife's hitbox while held also blocks projectiles.
WARNING: Ipecac's self-damage from explosions is lethal for The Lost, who dies in one hit. Even with Holy Mantle, one close-range shot breaks your shield. Extremely risky.
Epic Fetus's missile self-damage can kill The Lost. However, the crosshair-guided targeting lets you aim far from yourself. Powerful but requires very careful positioning.
Polyphemus's huge damage per shot is ideal for The Lost Ã¢â‚¬â€ kill enemies quickly before they can hit you. The slower fire rate is acceptable given The Lost's one-hit nature.
Sacred Heart's 2.3x damage multiplier plus homing is perfect for The Lost. Homing tears reduce the need to position dangerously, and the damage melts rooms quickly.
Technology fires a continuous laser alongside Azazel's Mini Brimstone. The laser fires while Brimstone charges, giving constant DPS. Good complement to Azazel's kit.
Adds variable charging to Azazel's Mini Brimstone. Tap for quick weak beams or hold for powerful charged blasts. Increases Azazel's flexibility.
Curved Horn adds +2 flat damage to every tear. Most impactful with multi-hit or rapid-fire builds where the flat bonus applies many times per second.
Perfection grants +10 Luck while held, destroyed on damage. Maximizes luck-based tear effects like Tough Love, Holy Light, and Parasitoid. Keep it safe for massive DPS boost.
Cancer reduces tear delay by 2, equivalent to a significant tears up. Benefits every build but especially strong with already-fast fire rates or charge-based items.
Multiplies the bonus from the highest stat up by 0.2 for other stats. Most powerful when you have one very high stat, spreading 20% of that bonus across all other stats.
Increases the fire rate of all familiars. Strongest with familiar-heavy builds (Incubus, Lil Brimstone, etc.) where the fire rate boost applies to multiple damage sources.
All familiar tears gain homing. Transforms every shooting familiar into a homing turret. Extremely valuable with multiple familiars like Incubus or Twisted Pair.
Forgotten Lullaby boosts Incubus's fire rate, making it shoot faster. Incubus copies your tear effects, so faster Incubus shots means faster delivery of all your tear modifiers.
Incubus tears gain homing from Baby-Bender. Since Incubus copies your damage and tear effects, homing Incubus tears are essentially a second set of homing shots.
Incubus fires tears that copy Isaac's damage and most tear effects. With Brimstone, it fires its own mini Brimstone. With Mom's Knife, it throws its own knife. Doubles your attack power.
WARNING: Soy Milk's damage multiplier stacks badly with Polyphemus. You get rapid fire but the damage penalty significantly undercuts Polyphemus's damage bonus. Generally a downgrade.
In Repentance, Isaac continuously fires bombs while charging Brimstone. You get both weapons active simultaneously Ã¢â‚¬â€ bombs during charge, beam on release.
Creates an energy orb that expands then shrinks while firing. You can charge a new beam immediately after. Unique mechanics but harder to aim, especially for Azazel.
WARNING: Tractor Beam forces the knife to snap to the firing direction, eliminating diagonal throwing. Reduces Mom's Knife's flexibility significantly.
WARNING: Creates a spiraling knife pattern that's very difficult to control. In some versions this makes Mom's Knife nearly unusable. Avoid this combination.
WARNING: Bob's Brain can easily self-damage The Lost with its explosion. Since The Lost dies in one hit, Bob's Brain is one of the most dangerous items to pick up.
WARNING: Kamikaze deals self-damage, which is instant death for The Lost. Completely useless and actively dangerous.
WARNING: Troll bombs spawn when The Lost takes damage (breaking Holy Mantle). Since The Lost is already in a vulnerable state, the bombs just add more danger to an already bad situation.
Doubles the effect of most tarot cards and suit cards. Extremely powerful with cards like The Sun (full heal + map), Strength (double stat boost), or The Stars (double Treasure Room teleport).
Re-activates your held card or rune on use. Combined with powerful cards like Jera (double all pickups), Perthro (infinite rerolls), or Algiz (invulnerability), this is game-breaking.
Re-activates your held pill on use. With stat-up pills (Tears Up, Damage Up), you can stack the same pill repeatedly for massive stat gains over a run.
The controllable Ludovico tear jitters wildly with random worm effects from Almond Milk, making it very hard to control. The rapid damage ticks compensate somewhat.
The targeting crosshair from Marked controls Epic Fetus missile targeting. Allows you to aim missiles independently of movement. Good for dodging while attacking.
Adds a chance to fire Brimstone beams from all four cardinal directions simultaneously. When it triggers, you get a 4-way laser cross. Devastating in crowded rooms.
Adds a chance to fire a Brimstone beam from the back of Isaac's head simultaneously. When it triggers, you fire forward and backward at once.
A second knife permanently exists behind Isaac, dealing contact damage. You get a rear-facing knife that hits enemies approaching from behind.
Occasionally releases 1-3 extra knives in random directions when shooting. Free extra knives on top of your main throw.
With the Guppy transformation active, Brimstone generates approximately one Blue Fly per tick per enemy hit, up to 13 flies. A single beam through multiple enemies creates an instant fly army.
With Guppy transformation, each tear has a chance to spawn Blue Flies. Soy Milk's rapid fire rate generates enormous waves of flies that overwhelm everything.
With Guppy transformation, the knife spawns Blue Flies frequently while contacting enemies. The knife's multi-hit nature generates flies rapidly.
Same principle as Soy Milk + Libra. Libra redistributes the tears bonus across all stats. The random worm effects from Almond Milk become less relevant when your stats are all boosted.
The technology laser curves back toward Isaac like a boomerang. Can hit enemies multiple times as the beam curves. Unique but inconsistent.
The knife returns to Isaac faster and has a tighter orbit. In practice, this concentrates the knife's damage at close range. Stat effects only, mostly neutral.
Enemies, pickups, and trinkets get drawn toward the crosshair. Clumps enemies together for the missile impact. Also conveniently gathers drops.
The Technology laser ticks much faster due to Soy Milk's fire rate boost. Combined with Soy Milk's own rapid tears, total DPS is significantly higher than either alone.
The Technology laser deals Polyphemus-boosted damage per tick. Slower tick rate from the fire rate penalty, but each tick hits much harder.
The laser deals 3x damage at point-blank range, decreasing with distance. Makes Technology devastating at close range but weaker at a distance.
Bombs release 10 knives in all directions when they explode. Each knife deals full damage. Turns every bomb into a knife nova.
Bombs explode into Technology beams in all directions. Combined with Tech X's ring damage, every bomb becomes a devastating multi-attack.
D6 rerolls pedestal items twice per use. Doubles your chances of finding an ideal item from any pedestal without spending extra charges.
Box of Friends activates twice, quadrupling all familiars for the room instead of doubling. With powerful familiars like Incubus or Twisted Pair, the damage output is absurd.
Grants two soul hearts per use instead of one. Doubles the survivability benefit of each activation while still guaranteeing the Horseman boss fight.
Grants two black hearts per use instead of one. In Repentance, also replaces the boss room item with a devil deal, so double activation gives even more value.
Spawns double the blue flies per use (4-8 instead of 2-4). With Guppy transformation active, generates a massive fly swarm on demand.
Mom's Box activates twice, spawning two trinkets. The trinket-doubling passive effect makes powerful trinkets like Curved Horn or Cancer even more impactful.
Sulfur activates twice, granting double Brimstone with a 1.2x damage multiplier for the current room. Extremely powerful temporary damage boost.
Car Battery's double-activation applies to every active item absorbed into Void. If Void has absorbed D6, Book of Revelations, etc., each triggers twice per use.
BFFS! doubles Incubus's tear damage. Since Incubus copies all your tear effects, a doubled Incubus is essentially a second you firing at 2x your damage.
Doubles the damage of both Twisted Pair familiars. They fire diagonally in front of you and copy your tear effects, creating massive supplemental DPS from three firing angles.
Doubles Lil Brimstone's mini laser damage. The familiar's Brimstone beam becomes a serious secondary attack that can clear weaker enemies on its own.
Increases the Succubus aura radius. The damage multiplier inside the aura remains the same, but the larger area makes it easier to stay buffed during combat.
Blue spiders from Infestation 2 deal doubled damage with Hive Mind. Each enemy killed spawns a powerful spider that chain-kills more enemies, snowballing through rooms.
Blue flies spawned by The Mulligan deal double damage with Hive Mind. High fire rate builds spawn many powerful flies that overwhelm rooms.

Shop items restock after purchase at half price. With two Steam Sales, items become completely free Ã¢â‚¬â€ infinite items from the shop. Even one Steam Sale makes shops extremely efficient with restocking.
Chaos merges all item pools into shops. With Restock keeping the shop stocked and Steam Sale(s) reducing prices, you can theoretically obtain every item in the game from the shop.
Sharp Plug fully charges your active item at the cost of health. The Wafer reduces all damage to half a heart. Net cost: half a heart per full charge, enabling repeated active item abuse.

At half a red heart, Scapular grants a soul heart. Taking damage breaks the soul heart and charges Habit, dropping you to half a heart again Ã¢â‚¬â€ re-triggering Scapular. Infinite active item charges from any damage source.
Brimstone's rapid damage ticks recharge your active item through 4.5 Volt. A single beam through multiple enemies can fully recharge even high-cost actives instantly.
Tech X rings hit enemies multiple times as they travel, generating rapid active item charges via 4.5 Volt. Keeps powerful actives available nearly every room.
The controllable Ludovico tear deals continuous contact damage, rapidly generating charges through 4.5 Volt. Park it on a boss for near-instant active item recharges.
Book of Belial spawns special wisps that grant a damage bonus while alive. The wisps fire tears and block shots, providing offense and defense alongside the damage up.
Satanic Bible creates special dark wisps that deal contact damage to enemies. Combined with the black heart from each use, provides both offensive wisps and survivability.
Absorbing D6 into Void gives you an on-demand reroll alongside any other absorbed effects. Using Void now rerolls all pedestal items in the room as a bonus.
Absorbing Mega Mush into Void adds its invincibility and size-up effect to every Void use. Repeatable room-long invulnerability with massive contact damage.
Dull Razor triggers on-hit effects without actually losing health. Bloody Lust stacks damage per hit taken. Use Dull Razor repeatedly to max out Bloody Lust damage at zero health cost.
Dull Razor counts as taking damage for Cambion Conception without losing health. Repeatedly use it to spawn demon familiars (Incubus, Succubus, etc.) for free.

Echo Chamber replays the last 3 cards used whenever you use a card. Combined with Blank Card, each activation cascades into multiple card effects. With powerful cards like Jera or The Sun, this breaks the game.
Using Diplopia in a shop that has Steam Sale duplicates it, giving you two Steam Sales. Two Steam Sales make all shop items free. Combine with Restock for infinite items.
Both are charge-based attacks. At full Brimstone charge, Maw of the Void also triggers its black damage ring around Isaac. You get the beam plus an automatic close-range ring attack every charge.
Holy Mantle negates the first hit per room, giving The Lost an effective second chance in every room. This is The Lost's starting item in Repentance and the foundation of all Lost runs.
Psy Fly orbits Isaac and deflects enemy projectiles back at enemies. For The Lost, this provides a massive defensive layer on top of Holy Mantle, blocking most incoming shots.
Dead Cat sets health to 1 heart. The Forgotten has bone hearts that count differently, and The Soul (his ghost form) still retains soul hearts. Less punishing than on most characters.
Blood Oath drains red hearts at the start of each floor, giving damage and speed. This triggers Whore of Babylon and Dead Bird automatically, stacking Eve's damage bonuses for the entire floor.
Money = Power adds damage based on coin count. Keeper naturally accumulates coins through his coin tears and Wooden Nickel. With 99 coins, this grants +3.96 damage.
Deep Pockets raises the coin cap to 999 and spawns coins in rooms with no other pickups. For Keeper, this means more potential healing pickups and higher Money = Power damage if also held.
Buying shop items grants random stat boosts. Keeper's coin-based economy makes frequent shop purchases natural, snowballing stats over the run.
IV Bag damages Keeper for a coin heart but drops coins. Each coin on the ground is a potential heal. With Swallowed Penny trinket, this becomes a free coin generator and heal loop.
Lilith starts with Incubus and Box of Friends. Each use doubles all familiars. With BFFS! or additional familiars, Box of Friends creates an army of high-damage copies every room.

Lilith frequently takes damage to charge Incubus. Cambion Conception spawns powerful demon familiars (Incubus, Dark Bum, Succubus) after taking enough hits, naturally synergizing with Lilith's playstyle.
Blue Baby cannot use red hearts. Dark Bum picks up red hearts and converts them to soul hearts, spiders, or other pickups. Turns useless red hearts into actual healing for Blue Baby.
Abaddon converts all red hearts to black hearts. Since Blue Baby has no red hearts, there's no downside Ã¢â‚¬â€ he just gets the +1.5 damage, speed up, and fear tears for free.

Bethany starts with Book of Virtues and can use soul hearts as active item charges. She generates wisps from every active use, building a massive orbital army of firing, shot-blocking wisps over the run.
Incubus is permanently attached to Lilith at close range, firing from her position. Removes the familiar delay and makes Incubus shots perfectly synchronized with your movement.
Book of Virtues wisps deal more damage and last longer. Strengthens Bethany's core mechanic, making her wisp army more durable and lethal.
Both brothers fuse together into a single body with combined stats. Eliminates the main weakness of controlling two characters and simplifies movement in tight rooms.
Gello fires tears from Lilith's body using her stats. Combined with Incubus and Box of Friends, Lilith can have multiple familiar clones all firing her tears simultaneously.
Incubus fires its own mini Brimstone beam alongside Lilith's blindfolded Brimstone. Two beams fire simultaneously, one from Lilith and one from Incubus. Box of Friends adds more beams.

WARNING: Abaddon converts all red heart containers to black hearts. Keeper uses coin hearts which count as red hearts Ã¢â‚¬â€ Abaddon removes ALL of Keeper's health, potentially killing him instantly.

Beelzebub transformation: collecting 3 fly-related items grants flight and converts enemy flies into blue flies. Any 3 fly items work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes BBF, Smart Fly, Jar of Flies, The Mulligan, Infestation 2, and others.

Bookworm transformation: collecting 3 book items grants a chance to fire double tears with each shot. Any 3 books work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes Book of Shadows, Book of Revelations, Satanic Bible, Book of Secrets, Book of Virtues, and others.

Seraphim transformation: collecting 3 angel room items grants flight and 3 soul hearts. Any 3 angel items work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes The Halo, Guardian Angel, Sworn Protector, Holy Mantle, Seraphim familiar, and others.

Leviathan transformation: collecting 3 devil room items grants flight and 2 black hearts. Any 3 devil items work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes The Mark, Spirit of the Night, Whore of Babylon, Abaddon, Maw of the Void, and others.
Spun transformation: collecting 3 syringe items grants +2 damage and +0.15 speed. Any 3 syringe items work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes Growth Hormones, Experimental Treatment, and various other syringes.
Conjoined transformation: collecting 3 specific familiar items causes Isaac to fire tears in 3 directions (forward and 2 diagonals). Applies a small damage penalty but triples coverage.
Fun Guy transformation: collecting 3 mushroom items grants +1 red heart container. Any 3 mushroom items work Ã¢â‚¬â€ includes Odd Mushroom (Large), 1up!, and other mushroom-themed items.
Oh Crap transformation: collecting 3 poop-related items causes destroying poop to heal half a red heart. Situational but provides consistent healing in many rooms.
Super Bum transformation: collecting all 3 bum familiars merges them into Super Bum. Super Bum collects all pickup types and gives high-value random rewards including cards, runes, and chests.

Swallowed Penny drops a coin whenever Keeper takes damage. Since coins heal Keeper, this effectively negates every hit Ã¢â‚¬â€ take damage, pick up the dropped coin to heal. Makes Keeper nearly unkillable.
Mom's Box doubles trinket effects while held. Curved Horn's +2 damage becomes +4 damage Ã¢â‚¬â€ a massive flat bonus that benefits rapid-fire builds enormously.
Mom's Box doubles Cancer trinket's tear delay reduction from -2 to -4. This is equivalent to a very large tears up, dramatically increasing fire rate on any build.
Mom's Box doubles Cracked Crown's stat multiplication bonus. The 20% stat spread becomes 40%, making your highest stat boost all other stats significantly.

Perfection grants +10 Luck while held, destroyed on taking damage. The Lost with Holy Mantle rarely takes unshielded hits, keeping Perfection active for most of the run. Maximizes luck-based tear effects.
Perfection's +10 Luck makes Tough Love's tooth tears fire on nearly every shot. Each tooth deals 3.2x damage, turning normal tears into a devastating rapid-fire assault.
Perfection's +10 Luck causes Holy Light's beam-of-light tears to trigger on nearly every shot. Each beam deals 3x your damage, turning every tear into a holy explosion.
Forgotten Lullaby boosts all familiar fire rates. Lilith relies entirely on familiars (Incubus, plus any from Box of Friends). Faster familiar fire rate directly multiplies Lilith's DPS.
Baby-Bender gives all familiar tears homing. Since Lilith fires through familiars only, this gives her entire offense homing Ã¢â‚¬â€ a massive accuracy boost with zero downside.
Forgotten Lullaby increases Twisted Pair's fire rate. Both familiars shoot faster, dramatically increasing the DPS from these powerful tear-copying familiars.
Baby-Bender grants homing to both Twisted Pair familiars. Their diagonal tears now track enemies, massively improving consistency and allowing them to hit targets Isaac's own tears might miss.
Mom's Purse lets you hold two trinkets. Curved Horn (+2 damage) and Cancer (-2 tear delay) together give a significant damage and fire rate boost. Other strong pairings work too.
WARNING: Cursed Eye teleports you to a random room when hit mid-charge. For The Lost, any hit is lethal anyway, but the teleport can send you to dangerous rooms. Avoid unless you have Black Candle.
WARNING: Missing No. randomizes all passive items every floor. This can destroy a winning run by replacing powerful items with useless ones. One of the most dangerous items to pick up.

WARNING: Eve's Mascara doubles damage but halves fire rate. Soy Milk massively increases fire rate but reduces damage. They partially cancel out, leaving you with mediocre damage and mediocre fire rate.
WARNING: Both items drastically reduce fire rate. The combined fire rate penalty is so severe that despite enormous per-tear damage, total DPS is often lower than base tears.
WARNING: Fires bombs at extremely high speed with drastically reduced damage per bomb. The rapid bombs fill the screen with low-damage explosions that can easily self-damage you.
WARNING: Isaac's Heart makes Azazel's body invulnerable but the heart familiar takes damage. Azazel's short-range Brimstone forces you close to enemies where the trailing heart is most vulnerable.
WARNING: Dead Eye's damage bonus resets on any miss. 20/20 fires two tears in a spread, making it easier for one tear to miss. The constant resets reduce Dead Eye's average damage boost.
WARNING: The Wiz fires tears diagonally, making it very hard to consistently hit targets. Dead Eye resets on every miss, so the diagonal spread keeps your damage multiplier permanently low.
Brimstone overrides Cursed Eye's charged burst, removing the teleportation risk entirely. You still get Brimstone's normal charge with no downside from Cursed Eye.
Mom's Knife overrides Cursed Eye's charged burst and removes the teleportation risk. You get the knife's normal behavior with no Cursed Eye downside.
Black Candle removes all curses, including Cursed Eye's teleportation effect when hit during charge. You keep the 4-tear charged burst with zero risk.
Death's Touch's +1.5 damage and piercing apply to Mom's Knife. The knife gains the damage boost and tears down doesn't matter since the knife has its own charge mechanic.
Death's Touch's +1.5 damage is added to Brimstone's beam damage. The tears down penalty doesn't affect Brimstone's charge speed. Pure damage upgrade.
Both grant a 1.5x damage multiplier, but they share the same multiplier and don't stack. You get the +3 flat damage from Magic Mushroom and all stats up, but only one 1.5x multiplier total.
Immaculate Heart's 1.2x damage multiplier applies to Brimstone. Additionally, orbital tears spawn and circle Isaac while the beam fires, adding extra hits to nearby enemies.
Both damage multipliers stack multiplicatively (2.3x from Sacred Heart, 1.2x from Immaculate Heart = 2.76x total). Combined with homing tears and orbital tears, this is devastating.
Star of Bethlehem is a familiar that slowly moves toward the boss room. While near it, Isaac gains a significant damage multiplier, tears up, and increased speed. Staying close to the star is key.

Damocles doubles all pedestal items but has a chance to instantly kill you after taking damage. Since The Lost dies in one hit anyway, the random death has no extra downside Ã¢â‚¬â€ pure double items.

WARNING: Damocles gives double items from every pedestal but creates a sword above you. After you take ANY damage, the sword has an increasing chance to fall and instantly kill you each half-second. High risk, high reward.
Stop Watch permanently slows all enemies and their projectiles. One of the strongest defensive items in the game. Makes every room and boss significantly easier to handle.
Sacred Orb prevents items with quality 0-1 from appearing, automatically rerolling them. Ensures every item pedestal offers quality 2+ items. Dramatically improves run consistency.
Item pedestals rapidly cycle between 5 different items. Picking up the pedestal gives whichever item is showing. Skilled players can consistently grab the best option from 5 choices.

Restarts the entire run from Basement I while keeping all your items. Essentially doubles the length of your run, letting you collect items from every floor twice. One of the rarest and most powerful items.
Teleports to a special area containing every item in the game Ã¢â‚¬â€ pick any one for free. Choose the single most powerful item you're missing (Sacred Heart, Godhead, R Key, etc.).
Magic Mushroom's 1.5x damage multiplier stacks with Polyphemus's massive damage. All stats up compensates somewhat for Polyphemus's fire rate penalty. Huge per-tear damage.
Cricket's Head's 1.5x damage multiplier applies on top of Polyphemus's damage formula. Combined, tears deal enormous damage that one-shots most non-boss enemies.
When both are held, Blood of the Martyr gains a hidden 1.5x damage multiplier that stacks with Book of Belial's damage boost. The combination provides much more damage than either alone.
All bombs fire 4 Brimstone lasers in cardinal directions on explosion. Every bomb placed or fired becomes a cross-shaped laser attack. Exceptionally strong with extra bomb items.
Dr. Fetus bomb shots each fire 4 Brimstone lasers in cardinal directions on detonation. Every shot creates a cross of lasers in addition to the explosion damage.
Bombs fire both 4 Brimstone lasers in cardinal directions AND 10 tears in all directions on explosion. Every bomb becomes an extreme room-clearing event.
Psy Fly orbits Isaac and homes in on enemy projectiles, deflecting them back as homing tears. Provides near-complete projectile immunity. One of the strongest defensive items in the game.
BFFS! increases Psy Fly's deflection damage. The deflected tears hit harder while maintaining the same excellent projectile blocking. Strong defensive and offensive combination.
9 Volt partially charges active items when entering a new room. Book of Belial is a 1-charge active, so it's available every single room Ã¢â‚¬â€ permanent +2 damage boost.
Guppy's Head is a 1-charge active, and 9 Volt grants 1 charge on room entry. This means infinite blue fly generation every room, even without clearing it.
The Battery lets active items double-charge. Blank Card with a double charge can activate your card twice in quick succession. With Jera, this quadruples all pickups.
D1 duplicates a random pickup in the room. Rune Bag drops runes every few rooms. Use D1 to duplicate powerful runes like Jera (doubling pickups) or Perthro (rerolling items) indefinitely.
Stand still for Gnawed Leaf invulnerability while Daddy Longlegs stomps enemies and Succubus's damage aura kills nearby enemies. Multiple auto-damage sources speed up the otherwise slow AFK strategy.
Fires arcing Brimstone balls that explode into multiple smaller Brimstone lasers on impact. Each laser deals full tick damage. Devastating area coverage.
Fires arcing knives that split into smaller knives on impact. The split knives inherit Mom's Knife damage. Creates a knife shower on each hit.
Fires arcing explosive tears that split into more explosive tears on impact. Each split tear also explodes. Chain explosions across the room. Extreme self-damage risk.
The main tear bursts into split tears on impact, and each split tear also splits again from Cricket's Body. Creates a cascade of projectiles that fills the room.
The controllable Ludovico tear periodically bursts and respawns, scattering smaller tears around it. Provides area damage around your controlled tear.
Trisagion beams gain Godhead's damaging aura. Since multiple beams can overlap on a single enemy, the aura damage stacks, dealing tremendous DPS at close range.
Trisagion's piercing light beams gain homing, curving toward enemies. The beams can hit the same enemy multiple times as they arc back, multiplying damage.
Trisagion beams increase in size and damage the further they travel. Since beams pierce and multi-hit, long-range Trisagion tears deal massive cumulative damage.
Electric arcs jump between the Brimstone beam's contact points and nearby enemies. Adds chain lightning damage on top of the beam's already high DPS.
Tech X rings generate electric arcs that jump to nearby enemies as they pass. Adds chain lightning damage to the ring's contact damage for wider area coverage.
The Technology laser generates constant electric arcs that jump to nearby enemies. Since the laser fires continuously, the lightning chains are constant, hitting groups of enemies.
Every tick of the Brimstone beam has a chance to summon a beam of holy light on the enemy. With a long beam hitting multiple enemies, this triggers frequently for massive bonus damage.
The beam sets enemies on fire without causing explosions. The burn damage stacks with beam damage ticks for additional DPS over time.
The laser turns yellow and each damage tick has a chance to drop a coin. Against rooms full of enemies, this generates significant income per beam.
Each beam tick has a 25% chance to spawn a spore on enemies. Spores burst and chain to nearby enemies, adding splash damage alongside the beam.
Leaves creep for each enemy or obstacle hit every other tick. Creates large pools of damaging creep that persist after the beam ends.
Rifts may appear where the beam hits rocks, walls, or enemies. The rifts pull in enemies and projectiles, adding crowd control alongside damage.
The beam has a chance to destroy rocks on each tick. Useful for opening paths and secret rooms while dealing damage.
Enemies killed by the beam are frozen solid. Frozen enemies can be kicked into other enemies for bonus damage.
The beam gains occasional damage multiplier procs from Apple!, applying to the whole beam. Luck-dependent but adds notable damage spikes.
Beam gains occasional 3.2x damage multiplier procs. At high luck this triggers very frequently, massively boosting beam DPS.
Each beam tick can mark enemies, causing them to be targeted by other enemies. Turns dense rooms into chaos as marked enemies get attacked by allies.
Shooting through Multidimensional Baby doubles the laser. Effectively creates a second beam for free if positioned correctly.
Any damage multiplier gained from Dead Eye before picking up Brimstone becomes permanent. A niche but powerful interaction if sequenced correctly.
The knife bounces as it flies away, with each bounce causing Isaac's tear damage. Adds extra hit opportunities at range.
Holy Light can trigger on both the held knife (contact damage) and the thrown knife. Constant contact damage means frequent holy beam procs.
The knife leaves creep trails while thrown. Creates damaging paths that persist after the knife returns.
The knife fires two knives in perpendicular directions from the first point of contact. Adds splash coverage to the knife's focused damage.
Isaac holds two knives that are shot diagonally. Doubles the knife output at the cost of cardinal direction aiming.
The knife fires toward the crosshair, charging automatically. Frees up movement controls for dodging while the knife auto-targets.
The knife turns green, poisons enemies, and can explode on enemy contact in Repentance. Adds explosive and poison damage to the knife.
Extra copies create additional knives firing at angles, stacking up to 16 maximum. Each Diplopia use adds another simultaneous knife.
Spin attack throws the sword a short distance while it spins. The sword gains a ranged component from the knife's charging mechanic.
Shoots a bomb surrounded by a laser ring. The ring deals Isaac's base damage while the bomb adds explosion damage. Double damage sources per shot.
Tears burst into 3-5 Tech X rings of various sizes on impact, each dealing 100% of Isaac's damage. Massive spread of laser rings.
Enemies drop money based on damage tick frequency. The ring's multi-hit nature generates significant coin income per target.
Every 15th laser ring fires a cluster of tears alongside it. Adds periodic burst damage to the consistent ring output.
Chance to fire 4 laser rings simultaneously in all cardinal directions. At high luck, triggers frequently for massive room coverage.
Isaac sometimes fires an additional ring from the back of his head. Free bonus damage output without downside.
Ring deals 4.38x damage near Isaac but decreases with distance. Extremely powerful at close range for aggressive playstyles.
Damaging halo lost but homing retained. The ring bends inward to hit enemies within it, substantially increasing the damage area.
Auto-charges and fires fully charged rings continuously. Creates a constant stream of full-size laser rings for sustained DPS.
Ring becomes poisonous and explodes every other tick on contact. Adds explosive damage to every tick of the ring for massive output.
Ring damage and size increase with distance. At maximum range deals approximately 1.5x damage, rewarding long-range play.
Damage multiplier from Dead Eye is preserved permanently. Missing shots don't remove it, making it a free damage boost.
Fires a continuous linear laser while charging Tech X. Provides constant chip damage during the charge phase.
Occasionally fires a linear laser while charging Tech X. Adds random burst damage between ring shots.
Fetuses are surrounded by laser rings dealing half their damage. Adds ring damage to every fetus projectile.
Adds 25 flat damage to bomb explosions. A straightforward and significant damage boost to every bomb shot.
Exploding bombs leave large pools of damaging creep. The creep persists and stacks, creating hazardous zones.
Shot bombs can be detonated on command. Allows precise timing for maximum damage placement against moving targets.
Bombs split into tiny bombs dealing half damage on detonation. Massively increases area coverage per shot.
Main tear scatters into 3-5 bombs that explode simultaneously. Massive area explosion coverage per shot.
Bombs gain homing, seeking enemies before detonating. Removes the main challenge of landing bomb shots accurately.
Fires a controllable bomb that detonates on command. Enables precise bomb placement at the cost of fire rate.
Bombs gain piercing and deal 2.5x piercing damage in Repentance+. Significantly increases damage against clustered enemies.
Bombs leave behind damaging fire on explosion. Adds persistent area denial to each bomb shot.
Black-tinted bombs summon Big Horn's hand to instantly kill non-boss enemies in Repentance+. Every bomb becomes a potential instant-kill.
Shoots rockets instead of bombs. With Polyphemus, rockets bounce off obstacles and detonate twice for double damage.
Shot bombs stick to enemies on contact, guaranteeing direct hits. In Repentance+, always spawns slowing white creep after exploding.
Fires 2 missiles per shot, doubling explosive output without any downside.
Fires 3 missiles per shot with reduced fire rate. Triple explosive coverage per volley for devastating room clears.
Fires 4 missiles per shot. Quadruple explosive coverage blankets entire rooms with reduced fire rate.
Incubus fires a missile alongside Isaac's, effectively doubling output. Two independent cursors for complete room coverage.
Each familiar fires missiles from additional crosshairs, tripling the missile output. Extra crosshairs move slower than Isaac's.
Luck-dependent chance to spawn four cardinal crosshairs mirroring the main position. At high luck, fires 5 missiles per shot.
Missile explosions have a chance of spawning pickups. Every room clear has potential for free resources.
Every missile explosion leaves a large lasting pool of red creep. Creates massive persistent damage zones.
The crosshair slowly slides toward enemies, making missile targeting semi-automatic. Reduces precision needed for hits.
Smaller tears orbit the main controllable tear. Orbiting tears deal additional damage to enemies near the main tear.
Turns the tear into a large bone that shoots spectral piercing tears when touching enemies. Adds splash damage to the controlled tear.
The tear becomes a large controllable laser ring dealing rapid damage at 1 tear delay rate. Full manual control of a Tech X-style ring.
The controlled tear releases holy beams periodically while touching an enemy. Constant bonus damage during sustained contact.
The controlled tear blocks enemy projectiles without disappearing. A mobile shield that also deals damage.
The tear becomes larger and deals more damage farther from Isaac. Encourages keeping distance while controlling the tear at range for max DPS.
Creep continuously oozes from underneath the tear wherever it moves. Creates permanent damage trails across the room.
A cluster of variably sized tears orbit the main tear, increasing the damage zone. No fire rate penalty since Ludovico takes priority.
Shot speed multiplier causes the tear to move significantly faster. Makes it easier to pursue fleeing enemies with the controlled tear.
The tear transforms into a tooth dealing 3.2x damage. At 9+ luck becomes permanent, making the controlled tear massively more powerful.
The tear bounces dealing splash damage per bounce. With Ipecac, each bounce causes a poisonous explosion.
While holding a direction key, the Ludovico tear constantly shoots controllable tears in random directions. Adds offensive output while controlling the main tear.
Bouncing shots create full-damage explosions instead of splash damage. Each bounce is a full Ipecac explosion for dramatically increased area damage.
Trisagion blasts become poisonous and explode on impact, dealing 10 plus twice the blast's damage. Excellent with Pyromaniac for self-healing.
The Maw ring becomes poisonous and explodes every other tick on contact. Adds Ipecac's explosive damage to the ring attack.
Tears orbit around Isaac before landing far enough away to avoid self-damage. One of the few modifiers that makes Ipecac safer to use.
Permanently grants the 6x fire rate multiplier from Kidney Stone after firing. Creates near-instant tear output permanently.
Red Stew's massive temporary +21.6 damage bonus becomes permanent. Locks in the full damage boost that normally decays over time.
All stat boosts from Purity remain active simultaneously. Grants permanent damage, fire rate, speed, and range boosts all at once.
Keeps Ipecac's massive +40 damage boost without the fire rate penalty. Combined with Brimstone or Tech X, damage becomes absurd.
Repeated D8 use only keeps the highest roll for each stat, yielding 1.5-2x higher values. Guaranteed improvement with each reroll.
Gets the 2x damage multiplier without fire rate or shot speed penalties. Pure damage boost with no downside.
Pills only apply damage increases, never the stat downsides. Turns every stat-down pill into a free damage up.
Speed penalties negated and all temporary stat boosts from Binge Eater become permanent. Free stats from every item pedestal.
Rock Bottom prevents the charge time increase, so Brimstone fires at near-normal fire rate. Dramatically increases beam DPS output.
Charged shots fire at normal rate since Rock Bottom prevents charge time increases. Full-power shots with rapid fire.
Tear rate overflow from Dataminer's random stat shuffles stays permanent. Can lock in 120+ fire rate forever.
Multiple tears without any stat downgrades. Pure multi-shot with zero penalties.
Sword becomes a lightsaber with a laser aura on every swing. Also deflects projectiles, combining offense and defense.
Sword and fired projectile become gigantic with massively increased damage. Charge time increases but damage is enormous.
Swing attack deals 9x Isaac's damage instead of 3x. One of the highest single-hit damage multipliers in the game.
Sword becomes larger. The projectile fires in an arc and splits on collision, creating a spread of burst tears alongside melee damage.
Both sword swings and fired projectiles can trigger Holy Light beams. Frequent melee hits mean frequent holy beam procs.
Sword swings reflect enemy projectiles back at them. Adds a powerful defensive ability to the melee weapon.
The fired sword projectile bursts into multiple smaller swords on impact. Adds splash coverage to the ranged attack.
Random chance to swing swords in all 4 cardinal directions. At high luck, creates devastating 4-way melee sweeps.
Fired projectile becomes a Trisagion blast that pierces and deals continuous damage. Increases ranged attack utility significantly.
Isaac spins one additional time per extra tear, firing extra projectiles on each spin. Doubles projectile output of charged attacks.
Fetuses hold small swords and perform charged spin attacks as they travel. Each fetus becomes a miniature sword-wielding attacker.
A Technology laser connects Isaac to the thrown sword while in flight. The laser deals continuous damage along its entire path.
Launches a barrage of knives from Isaac's mouth after doing the spin attack. Combines melee with a ranged projectile burst.
Fires arcing tears that release Technology lasers in all directions upon impact. Each burst creates a starburst of piercing laser beams.
Charged shots and burst tears become larger with higher damage. Enables rapid low-damage bursts or devastating fully-charged explosive tears.
Main tears split on enemy/obstacle hits. Split tears can burst but don't split further, creating cascading projectiles.
Both main and burst tears increase the damage bonus. Missing burst tears doesn't reset the multiplier, making max damage easy to maintain.
Main tear bounces releasing burst tears on each bounce, with multiple bounces at high range. In Repentance, burst tears also bounce.
Lead Pencil fires 12 Haemolacria tears, all of which burst on impact. Creates an enormous volley of splitting projectiles.
Fires a burst of 4-7 Haemolacria tears, all bursting on impact. Creates a screen-filling cascade of projectiles.
Creates large tears that burst into standard tears unaffected by Proptosis damage falloff. The initial hit deals massive close-range damage.
Creates a large Trisagion beam that splits into more beams at maximum range. Combines piercing with burst mechanics.
Fully charged blasts deal 4x more damage. High-reward charged piercing blasts that cut through entire rooms.
Creates large stacking creep trails from each piercing hit. Deals significant passive damage as creep pools overlap heavily.
Lengthens blasts into flexible beams with larger damage areas. Increases the effective hitbox of each shot.
Every damage tick shoots a Pop! tear. Trisagion shots hover longer than normal, creating a field of bouncing tears.
Blasts deal 2x damage instead of the normal 0.33x, with minimal distance falloff. Massively boosts Trisagion's base damage.
Original blast shoots four white tears crosswise during travel and at range end. Adds splash to the piercing beam.
Small electric arcs generate from the blast every tick. Constant electricity throughout the beam's path.
Spores stack multiple times on enemies due to the multi-hit nature. Creates rapid spore chain reactions.
Beam becomes a controllable Trisagion blast with high hit rate. Stays on enemies dealing continuous piercing damage.
Tiny tear bombs deal normal explosion damage despite Soy Milk's penalty. Massive fire rate creates constant sticky bombs on enemies.
Massive tear barrage greatly increases coin drop chance. Generates significant income from every room clear.
Generates enormous blue fly swarms from high tear volume. With Guppy transformation, creates a screen-filling army of flies.
Creates stacking creep damage pools with each rapid-fire shot. Quickly fills rooms with overlapping damage zones.
Greatly increases knockback on rapid-fire tears, pinning enemies against walls. Turns the tear stream into a crowd control tool.
Fetuses become skeletons firing splitting bone fragments on enemy contact. Adds splash projectiles to every fetus hit.
Fetuses drop four short-ranged tears on enemy contact. Adds area splash to each fetus's attack.
Fetuses drop small bombs on enemy contact dealing half fetus damage. Combines homing with explosive area damage.
Fetuses zap nearby enemies with electric arcs on contact. Adds chain lightning damage to every fetus hit.
Grants +5 flat damage per fetus without fire rate penalty. Pure damage boost with no downside.
Fetuses rapidly shoot short-ranged lasers facing enemies. Adds continuous laser output to each homing fetus.
Massive tears block enemy shots and continue, losing only 10 damage per blocked projectile. Acts as a shield that barely loses damage.
Both main and burst tears maintain all Godhead effects including the damaging aura. Splitting tears multiply the number of active auras.
Both main and split tears maintain the damaging aura and homing. Creates cascading homing aura-bearing projectiles.
Split tears maintain all Godhead effects. Perpendicular splits create additional aura damage zones.
The enlarged tear gains homing and a much larger, more powerful damaging aura. Massive single-shot damage potential.
All tears in the burst bounce simultaneously with greatly increased range. The bouncing barrage covers a much larger area.
Tears stay airborne longer and return to Isaac. Better reach and more focused blasts before the boomerang return.
All tears follow the beam without spreading. Much easier for the entire burst to hit a single target.
All tears are larger with increased damage instead of alternating. In Afterbirth+, 50% chance per tear for Blood Clot bonus.
Fires many extra tears alongside the burst that begin orbiting Isaac. Creates both a projectile burst and an orbital shield.
In Repentance+, chance to fire full barrages in all cardinal directions. At high luck, fires 4 simultaneous bursts.
In Repentance+, chance to fire a full barrage from the back of Isaac's head. Doubles output when triggered.
Fires automatically at full charge with random worm effects. Continuous barrages of tears with chaotic but effective coverage.
Lasers bounce off walls once, creating a second beam from the impact point. Doubles room coverage in enclosed spaces.
In Repentance+, the laser wraps through walls with momentum retained. Allows hitting enemies through any wall.
Laser becomes dark purple with a controllable crosshair. Allows precise directional control of the beam.
Tears that wrap through walls keep accumulating distance-based damage. Tears travel nearly endlessly, gaining massive damage bonuses.
Toxic Shock poisons all enemies on room entry. With Polyphemus damage, the poison kills nearly all non-boss enemies instantly upon entering.
Sacrificial Dagger orbits dealing high contact damage while Gnawed Leaf provides invincibility. One of the best AFK combos due to Dagger's damage.
Activate Dull Razor at room start to trigger on-hit effects without damage, then go invincible. Works with Bloody Lust, Smart Fly, and other on-hit synergies.
Box of Friends duplicates both Twisted Pair familiars. Each use spawns two more at greater orbit distances, quadrupling familiar tear output.
Familiars' tears follow the beam's trajectory. Focuses all familiar fire into a single devastating line of damage.
Creates a Godhead aura around the thrown head with homing. The aura damages enemies on the head's flight path.
Damaging aura expands once Explosivo attaches to an enemy. Growing aura damages nearby enemies while the explosive charges.
Damaging aura expands when Mucormycosis attaches. Growing auras and spore bursts create chain reactions.
In Repentance+, laser creates 4-way tear spreads on wall or enemy hits. Adds splash projectiles to the piercing beam.
In Repentance+, laser splits into perpendicular beams on obstacle hits. Creates T-shaped laser patterns for wider coverage.
